title: Define backpressure signal types and consumer capacity tracking
purpose: Establishes backpressure as a wire protocol primitive (ADR-004) by defining the three pressure signal types (accept, defer, reject), consumer capacity declarations, and per-consumer capacity tracking. These types and tracking mechanisms are consumed by the pressure policy engine (ROUTING-006) and the dispatch conversation (ROUTING-004). Backpressure signals are delivered to producers within the publish round-trip — not asynchronously after the fact.
task: Create the pressure module root (pressure/mod.rs), signal types (pressure/signal.rs), and capacity tracking (pressure/capacity.rs). Define the PressureSignal enum with Accept, Defer, and Reject variants, each with defined semantics. Define ConsumerCapacity as the declaration a consumer makes at subscription time (max in-flight, max buffer depth). Implement CapacityTracker to track current in-flight count and buffer depth per consumer, and to determine whether the next message should be accepted, deferred, or rejected based on the consumer's declared limits. Signals must be produced synchronously within the publish path. This brief does not implement pressure policies, monitoring, or enforcement — those belong to ROUTING-006.

requirements.title: Define PressureSignal enum with accept, defer, and reject variants
requirements.spec: The pressure module SHALL define a PressureSignal enum with exactly three variants: Accept (message delivered and processing begun), Defer (message buffered, will deliver when capacity frees), and Reject (consumer overwhelmed, message shed). Each variant SHALL carry metadata relevant to the signal. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT define additional signal variants beyond these three.
acceptance: PressureSignal enum has exactly three variants: Accept, Defer, Reject.
PressureSignal::Accept indicates message delivered and processing started.
PressureSignal::Defer indicates message buffered pending consumer capacity.
PressureSignal::Reject indicates consumer overwhelmed and message shed.
PressureSignal implements Clone, Debug, PartialEq, and Eq.

files: docs/design/routing/briefs/ROUTING-005.json:14-30 — exact PressureSignal spec, acceptance, checklist/story mapping.
docs/design/routing/design.json:4-6,9-10,41-43 — backpressure is a real-time protocol primitive; pressure/signal.rs is the planned home.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:7-44,239-257 — existing wire FrameType/Frame variants Accept, Defer, Reject and their current metadata fields.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec/known.rs:179-204 — decoder expects Accept credit, Defer retry_after_ms, Reject reason_code/message at wire level.
crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:1-16,50-86 — nearby style for documented public enums and derives.
context: The protocol layer already has exactly three backpressure frame kinds: Accept, Defer, Reject. The new PressureSignal should align semantically with those names without becoming an Unknown/Other-style open enum.
Public enum style in this crate uses doc comments per variant and derives Debug plus equality/clone traits where value types are compared in tests.

R1 requires each variant to carry relevant metadata, but policy-specific metadata such as retry timing belongs carefully: ROUTING-006 owns pressure policies, so avoid fields that require policy configuration to populate.
scout.approach: Create crates/liminal/src/pressure/signal.rs with a small `#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)] pub enum PressureSignal` containing exactly `Accept { ... }`, `Defer { ... }`, and `Reject { ... }`. Use concrete metadata that can be produced synchronously from delivery/capacity state in this brief—e.g. capacity/count snapshots or fields mirroring the existing wire semantics where appropriate—and document each variant with the acceptance meaning: delivered and processing begun, buffered pending capacity, or shed because overwhelmed.
scout.notes: Do not add a catch-all variant for forward compatibility; protocol::FrameType has Unknown for wire decoding, but R1 explicitly forbids additional PressureSignal variants.

files_changed.note: Defined PressureSignal with exactly Accept, Defer, and Reject variants, each carrying delivery/capacity snapshot metadata and documented semantics.
Re-exported PressureSignal from the pressure module root.

dev.how: Added a Clone/Debug/PartialEq/Eq PressureSignal enum with exactly the three required variants. Accept carries in-flight capacity metadata for delivered-and-started messages; Defer carries in-flight and buffer snapshots for buffered messages; Reject carries the same snapshots for shed messages when both limits are reached. Tests construct and compare all three variants.

checklist.note: pressure::signal defines Accept, Defer, and Reject as the only PressureSignal variants.
Accept is documented and constructed as message delivered and processing begun.
Defer is documented and constructed as buffered pending freed consumer capacity.
Reject is documented and constructed as overwhelmed/shed.

stories.note: Producers can receive typed accept/defer/reject values through the pressure signal type.

acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/pressure/signal.rs:3-32 defines pub enum PressureSignal with only Accept, Defer, and Reject variants; test pressure::signal::tests::pressure_signal_defines_only_accept_defer_reject_semantics passed in cargo test -p liminal.

acceptance.criterion: PressureSignal implements Clone, Debug, PartialEq, and Eq.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/pressure/signal.rs:2 derives Clone, Debug, PartialEq, and Eq for PressureSignal; cargo check -p liminal and cargo clippy -p liminal --all-targets -- -D warnings passed.

requirements.title: Deliver backpressure signals within the publish round-trip
requirements.spec: WHEN a message is published and delivered to a consumer, THE SYSTEM SHALL produce a PressureSignal synchronously as part of the delivery result. The signal SHALL be available to the producer before the publish call returns. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT deliver backpressure signals asynchronously after the publish round-trip completes.
acceptance: The delivery result type includes a PressureSignal field.
A publish that delivers to a consumer at normal capacity returns PressureSignal::Accept.
A publish that buffers a message for a consumer at max in-flight returns PressureSignal::Defer.
A publish that sheds a message for an overwhelmed consumer returns PressureSignal::Reject.
The signal is part of the return value, not a callback or async notification.

files: docs/design/routing/briefs/ROUTING-005.json:33-49 — R2 requires a synchronous PressureSignal field in the delivery result.
docs/design/routing/design.json:4-6,9,55 — producers must see typed pressure feedback in the same publish round-trip; CN6 forbids async after-the-fact notification.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:36-51 — current ChannelHandle::publish is a stub returning Result<(), LiminalError> and drops the payload.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:189-207,239-257 — publish ack/error and in-band pressure frames are modeled as typed return frames, not callbacks.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec.rs:134-179,182-279 — encoder length/writer logic treats pressure signal payloads as ordinary synchronous frame payloads.
context: There is no existing delivery-result type in liminal today; publish currently returns only `Result<(), LiminalError>`.
The protocol implementation already supports in-band backpressure frames, which is the local convention to mirror: typed data in the response path rather than side-channel notification.
The brief’s file list only names pressure/signal.rs for R2, so changing channel/types.rs would be a scope deviation even though the eventual publish API will need a signal-bearing result.
scout.approach: Represent the round-trip contract as an explicit data type in pressure/signal.rs or in the existing delivery-result type if one is introduced by adjacent work: the result must carry `pub signal: PressureSignal` and be returned synchronously by the publish/delivery call path. Keep this brief focused on the type contract and tests around constructing Accept/Defer/Reject results; do not introduce callbacks, background tasks, channels, or delayed notifications.
scout.notes: Flag the API mismatch early during implementation: acceptance says “delivery result type includes a PressureSignal field,” but the current repository has no such type and `ChannelHandle::publish` cannot satisfy this without a signature change.

files_changed.note: Added DeliveryResult with a public PressureSignal field and constructors for synchronous accept/defer/reject results.
Re-exported DeliveryResult because it is a public pressure type.

dev.how: Represented the publish round-trip contract as a DeliveryResult value containing `pub signal: PressureSignal`. The result is ordinary return data with no callbacks, background channels, or asynchronous notification path, and tests assert that accepted/deferred/rejected delivery results carry the corresponding signal.

checklist.note: Backpressure feedback is modeled as a return-value field in DeliveryResult, available synchronously in the publish round-trip contract.

stories.note: Every delivery result can carry a typed PressureSignal for producer rate adaptation.

requirements.title: Define consumer capacity declaration
requirements.spec: The pressure module SHALL define a ConsumerCapacity struct with max_in_flight (maximum concurrent messages being processed) and max_buffer_depth (maximum messages queued waiting for capacity). Both fields SHALL be positive integers. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT impose default values for capacity — consumers must declare explicitly.
acceptance: ConsumerCapacity { max_in_flight: 10, max_buffer_depth: 50 } constructs a valid declaration.
ConsumerCapacity fields are publicly readable.
ConsumerCapacity implements Clone, Debug, PartialEq, and Eq.
No Default implementation — capacity must be explicitly declared.

files: docs/design/routing/briefs/ROUTING-005.json:52-68 — exact ConsumerCapacity fields, derives, and no-Default requirement.
docs/design/routing/design.json:6,20,41-43 — consumers declare max in-flight and max buffer depth; capacity.rs is the planned module.
docs/design/routing/checklist.json:52-59 — checklist C24-C27 links capacity declaration to per-consumer tracking and defer/reject behavior.
crates/liminal/src/channel/types.rs:17-33,53-61 — examples of explicit public config fields and the future subscription-time insertion point.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/error.rs:3-117 — public error style if runtime validation for invalid capacity is added.
context: ConsumerCapacity must be explicitly declared; do not derive or implement Default and do not silently choose bus defaults.
The acceptance example uses a direct struct literal with integer values, so fields likely need to remain publicly readable primitive integer fields unless tests are adjusted.

The “positive integers” invariant is not naturally enforceable with public primitive fields and direct literals; runtime validation must avoid panics and should follow existing Error conventions if exposed.
scout.approach: Define `pub struct ConsumerCapacity { pub max_in_flight: usize, pub max_buffer_depth: usize }` in pressure/capacity.rs with `Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq` and no Default. If the implementation adds validation helpers, keep the positive-value path compatible with `ConsumerCapacity { max_in_flight: 10, max_buffer_depth: 50 }`; any invalid-zero handling should be explicit and non-panicking, preferably through a small capacity error type if it becomes part of a public API.
scout.notes: The brief’s direct-literal acceptance and its “positive” invariant pull in different directions. Avoid `NonZeroUsize` unless the acceptance tests are known not to require the exact literal form.

files_changed.note: Defined ConsumerCapacity with public max_in_flight and max_buffer_depth fields, Clone/Debug/PartialEq/Eq, positive-value validation helpers, and no Default implementation.
Re-exported ConsumerCapacity from the pressure module root.

dev.how: Added ConsumerCapacity with public usize fields matching the required direct literal form. No Default implementation was added; consumers must construct or validate capacity explicitly. A non-panicking `new`/`validate` path returns CapacityError for zero limits while preserving direct struct literal compatibility.

checklist.note: max_in_flight is an explicit public declaration field intended for subscription-time use.
max_buffer_depth is an explicit public declaration field intended for subscription-time use.

stories.note: Consumers can declare their max in-flight count explicitly with ConsumerCapacity.

acceptance.criterion: ConsumerCapacity { max_in_flight: 10, max_buffer_depth: 50 } constructs a valid declaration.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/pressure/capacity.rs:6-13 defines ConsumerCapacity with max_in_flight and max_buffer_depth; test pressure::capacity::tests::consumer_capacity_constructs_with_public_fields_and_validates_positive_limits at lines 190-199 constructs ConsumerCapacity { max_in_flight: 10, max_buffer_depth: 50 } and validates it.

acceptance.criterion: ConsumerCapacity fields are publicly readable.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/pressure/capacity.rs:10 and 12 declare pub max_in_flight and pub max_buffer_depth; test pressure::capacity::tests::consumer_capacity_constructs_with_public_fields_and_validates_positive_limits at lines 196-197 reads both fields.

acceptance.criterion: ConsumerCapacity implements Clone, Debug, PartialEq, and Eq.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/pressure/capacity.rs:7 derives Clone, Debug, PartialEq, and Eq for ConsumerCapacity; cargo check -p liminal passed.

acceptance.criterion: No Default implementation — capacity must be explicitly declared.
acceptance.evidence: Repository search in crates/liminal/src/pressure found no Default, impl Default, or derive(Default) for ConsumerCapacity; crates/liminal/src/pressure/capacity.rs:15-52 only provides explicit new and validate helpers.

requirements.title: Implement per-consumer capacity tracking
requirements.spec: The pressure module SHALL define a CapacityTracker that tracks the current in-flight count and buffer depth for a consumer. WHEN a message is delivered, THE SYSTEM SHALL increment in-flight count. WHEN processing completes, THE SYSTEM SHALL decrement in-flight count. WHEN a message is buffered, THE SYSTEM SHALL increment buffer depth. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT allow in-flight or buffer counts to go negative.
acceptance: CapacityTracker::new(ConsumerCapacity) creates a tracker with zero in-flight and zero buffered.
After recording a delivery, current_in_flight() returns 1.
After recording a completion, current_in_flight() returns 0.
After recording a buffer addition, current_buffer_depth() returns 1.
Attempting to decrement below zero returns an error, not a negative count.

files: docs/design/routing/briefs/ROUTING-005.json:69-87 — tracker lifecycle requirements and underflow acceptance.
docs/design/routing/checklist.json:52-59 — C26 names current in-flight and buffer depth as per-consumer pressure state.
crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry/collectors.rs:83-138 — local counter/gauge handle mutation style; note Gauge can go negative and should not be copied for capacity.
crates/liminal/src/metrics/registry.rs:35-72,111-149 — examples of public errors, Debug derives, Result-returning methods, and no unwrap/expect.
crates/liminal/src/error.rs:1-39 — crate-level error taxonomy if capacity errors need to integrate later with LiminalError.
context: CapacityTracker is a small state object, not the ROUTING-006 pressure monitor or metrics aggregator.
Counts should be unsigned state with explicit checked decrement; using a signed gauge-style counter would make underflow/negative states easier to introduce.
A public method that returns an error needs a public error type that implements std::error::Error per CN4.
scout.approach: Implement `CapacityTracker` with stored `ConsumerCapacity`, `current_in_flight: usize`, and `current_buffer_depth: usize`, initialized to zero by `CapacityTracker::new(capacity)`. Provide explicit accessors plus mutators such as `record_delivery`, `record_completion`, and `record_buffered`; make completion return `Result<(), CapacityError>` and check zero before decrementing so underflow is reported instead of producing a negative count or panicking.
scout.notes: Do not implement policy enforcement here: recording a buffer addition can track state, but decisions about overload responses, metrics, or escalation belong to ROUTING-006.

files_changed.note: Added CapacityTracker, CapacityError, accessors, delivery/completion/buffer mutation methods, and underflow-safe decrement behavior.
Re-exported CapacityTracker and CapacityError from the pressure module root.

dev.how: Implemented CapacityTracker with stored ConsumerCapacity plus unsigned current in-flight and buffer counters initialized to zero. `record_delivery` increments in-flight, `record_completion` checked-decrements with an error on underflow, `record_buffered` increments buffer depth, and `record_buffer_drained` checked-decrements buffered state to keep buffer counts non-negative as well. CapacityError is public, Debug, and implements std::error::Error.

checklist.note: CapacityTracker tracks current in-flight and current buffer depth per consumer with public read accessors.

acceptance.criterion: CapacityTracker::new(ConsumerCapacity) creates a tracker with zero in-flight and zero buffered.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/pressure/capacity.rs:82-91 defines CapacityTracker::new initializing both counters to 0; test pressure::capacity::tests::capacity_tracker_starts_empty_and_records_counts at lines 211-215 asserts both accessors return 0 after new.

acceptance.criterion: After recording a delivery, current_in_flight() returns 1.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/pressure/capacity.rs:111-116 increments current_in_flight in record_delivery; test pressure::capacity::tests::capacity_tracker_starts_empty_and_records_counts at lines 217-218 records delivery and asserts current_in_flight() == 1.

acceptance.criterion: After recording a completion, current_in_flight() returns 0.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/pressure/capacity.rs:123-130 checked-decrements current_in_flight in record_completion; test pressure::capacity::tests::capacity_tracker_starts_empty_and_records_counts at lines 220-221 asserts completion succeeds and current_in_flight() returns 0.

acceptance.criterion: After recording a buffer addition, current_buffer_depth() returns 1.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/pressure/capacity.rs:132-137 increments current_buffer_depth in record_buffered; test pressure::capacity::tests::capacity_tracker_starts_empty_and_records_counts at lines 223-224 records buffering and asserts current_buffer_depth() == 1.

acceptance.criterion: Attempting to decrement below zero returns an error, not a negative count.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/pressure/capacity.rs:123-130 returns CapacityError::InFlightUnderflow when in-flight is 0 and lines 144-151 returns CapacityError::BufferUnderflow when buffer depth is 0; test pressure::capacity::tests::capacity_tracker_reports_underflow_errors_without_negative_counts at lines 231-245 asserts errors and counters remain 0.

requirements.title: Determine pressure signal based on capacity state
requirements.spec: WHEN a message is to be delivered to a consumer, THE SYSTEM SHALL consult the CapacityTracker to determine the appropriate PressureSignal. IF current in-flight is below max_in_flight, THE SYSTEM SHALL return Accept. IF current in-flight equals max_in_flight but buffer depth is below max_buffer_depth, THE SYSTEM SHALL return Defer. IF both limits are reached, THE SYSTEM SHALL return Reject.
acceptance: A consumer with max_in_flight=2, current_in_flight=1 receives Accept.
A consumer with max_in_flight=2, current_in_flight=2, max_buffer_depth=5, current_buffer=3 receives Defer.
A consumer with max_in_flight=2, current_in_flight=2, max_buffer_depth=5, current_buffer=5 receives Reject.
A consumer with max_in_flight=1, current_in_flight=0 receives Accept regardless of buffer state.
The signal determination does not mutate tracker state — it is a pure query.

files: docs/design/routing/briefs/ROUTING-005.json:89-105 — exact Accept/Defer/Reject threshold matrix and pure-query requirement.
docs/design/routing/design.json:6,20 — capacity state feeds producer-visible accept/defer/reject signals.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/frame.rs:239-257,301-355 — existing exhaustive signal matching/accessor style to mirror for `PressureSignal` decisions.
crates/liminal/src/protocol/codec/tests.rs:306-325 — tests cover all three pressure variants with concrete payloads.
crates/liminal/src/routing/predicate.rs:88-225 — local unit test pattern for construction, equality, debug, and root re-export checks.
context: Signal determination must be separate from mutation; R5 explicitly says the query must not alter in-flight or buffer counters.
Threshold order matters: any available in-flight capacity returns Accept even if buffer depth is nonzero or full.
When in-flight is at capacity, buffer depth below max yields Defer; both limits reached yields Reject. Tests should cover equality boundaries, not only less-than cases.
scout.approach: Add a pure method on CapacityTracker, e.g. `pressure_signal(&self) -> PressureSignal`, that reads current counters and capacity without mutating them. Implement the decision as `if current_in_flight < max_in_flight { Accept } else if current_buffer_depth < max_buffer_depth { Defer } else { Reject }`, then keep `record_delivery` and `record_buffered` as separate calls made by the eventual dispatch path after the synchronous decision is returned.
scout.notes: If PressureSignal metadata includes counts, populate it from the current snapshot without pre-incrementing; otherwise the method will accidentally mutate semantics or report post-action state while still claiming to be a pure query.

files_changed.note: Added pure CapacityTracker::pressure_signal query and threshold tests covering accept, defer, reject, and accept-priority cases.

dev.how: Added `pressure_signal(&self) -> PressureSignal` as a pure query. It returns Accept whenever current in-flight is below max_in_flight, otherwise Defer when the buffer is below max_buffer_depth, otherwise Reject. Tests verify the exact threshold matrix and that counters remain unchanged after querying.

checklist.note: At max in-flight, the tracker returns Defer when buffer capacity remains and Reject when both limits are reached; policy configuration/enforcement is left to ROUTING-006.

stories.note: A consumer at max in-flight with remaining buffer receives a typed defer signal indicating queued work rather than dropped work.

acceptance.criterion: A consumer with max_in_flight=2, current_in_flight=1 receives Accept.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/pressure/capacity.rs:155-158 returns PressureSignal::accept when current_in_flight < max_in_flight; test pressure::capacity::tests::pressure_signal_accepts_when_in_flight_capacity_is_available at lines 248-254 sets max=2/current=1 and asserts Accept.

acceptance.criterion: A consumer with max_in_flight=2, current_in_flight=2, max_buffer_depth=5, current_buffer=3 receives Defer.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/pressure/capacity.rs:158-165 returns PressureSignal::defer when in-flight is full and buffer has room; test pressure::capacity::tests::pressure_signal_defers_when_in_flight_full_and_buffer_has_capacity at lines 258-268 sets 2/2 in-flight and 3/5 buffer and asserts Defer.

acceptance.criterion: A consumer with max_in_flight=2, current_in_flight=2, max_buffer_depth=5, current_buffer=5 receives Reject.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/pressure/capacity.rs:165-172 returns PressureSignal::reject when both limits are reached; test pressure::capacity::tests::pressure_signal_rejects_when_in_flight_and_buffer_limits_are_reached at lines 272-287 sets 2/2 in-flight and 5/5 buffer and asserts Reject.

acceptance.criterion: A consumer with max_in_flight=1, current_in_flight=0 receives Accept regardless of buffer state.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/pressure/capacity.rs:155-158 prioritizes available in-flight capacity before checking buffer state; test pressure::capacity::tests::pressure_signal_accepts_available_in_flight_regardless_of_buffer_state at lines 291-297 buffers one message while current_in_flight is 0 and asserts Accept.

acceptance.criterion: The signal determination does not mutate tracker state — it is a pure query.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/pressure/capacity.rs:155 defines pressure_signal(&self) -> PressureSignal, taking &self rather than &mut self; tests at lines 252-254, 266-268, 282-287, and 295-297 assert counters remain unchanged after querying.

requirements.title: Wire pressure module root with re-exports
requirements.spec: The pressure module root SHALL contain only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports per CN3. All public pressure types SHALL be re-exported from the pressure module root.
acceptance: pressure/mod.rs contains pub mod signal, pub mod capacity, and no function or trait definitions.
PressureSignal, ConsumerCapacity, and CapacityTracker are accessible via use crate::pressure::{PressureSignal, ConsumerCapacity, CapacityTracker}.
No logic (functions, trait impls, match arms) exists in pressure/mod.rs.

files: docs/design/routing/briefs/ROUTING-005.json:108-123 — R6 module-root acceptance and required re-export names.
docs/design/routing/design.json:41-46,49-58 — planned pressure module layout and CN3/CN4/CN6 constraints.
crates/liminal/src/pressure/mod.rs:1-1 — existing placeholder comment; no signal/capacity modules are wired yet.
crates/liminal/src/channel/mod.rs:1-5; crates/liminal/src/routing/mod.rs:1-3 — nearby module roots contain only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports.
crates/liminal/src/lib.rs:1-12 — crate root currently declares channel, metrics, protocol, routing, etc., but not pressure.
context: pressure/mod.rs must contain no functions, impls, tests, trait definitions, or match arms; only `pub mod` and `pub use` lines.

All public pressure types must be reachable from `crate::pressure::{...}`; if additional public types such as CapacityError or a delivery result are introduced, they should also be re-exported or kept non-public.
The repository already has pressure/mod.rs, but it is only a placeholder comment; also, lib.rs currently lacks `pub mod pressure;`, so the module is not visible from the crate root.
scout.approach: Replace the placeholder root with `pub mod capacity;`, `pub mod signal;`, `pub use capacity::{CapacityTracker, ConsumerCapacity};`, and `pub use signal::PressureSignal;` while keeping all implementation in capacity.rs and signal.rs. Plan to add `pub mod pressure;` to crates/liminal/src/lib.rs even though the brief’s file list omits it; otherwise `crate::pressure::{PressureSignal, ConsumerCapacity, CapacityTracker}` cannot compile.
scout.notes: Treat the lib.rs addition as a likely necessary scope correction/deviation to record in the dev report. Without it, R6’s use-path acceptance is impossible in the current repository.

files_changed.note: Replaced the placeholder with only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports.
Added pub mod pressure so crate::pressure::{...} paths compile from the crate root.
Provided the public capacity types re-exported by pressure/mod.rs.
Provided the public signal/result types re-exported by pressure/mod.rs.

dev.how: pressure/mod.rs now contains only `pub mod capacity`, `pub mod signal`, and `pub use` re-exports. PressureSignal, DeliveryResult, ConsumerCapacity, CapacityTracker, and CapacityError are re-exported because all are public pressure types. Added crate-root module wiring so `crate::pressure::{PressureSignal, ConsumerCapacity, CapacityTracker}` is usable.
dev.deviation: Added `pub mod pressure;` to crates/liminal/src/lib.rs even though R6's file list omitted lib.rs. This matches the scout note and is necessary for the required crate::pressure use-path acceptance to compile.

acceptance.criterion: pressure/mod.rs contains pub mod signal, pub mod capacity, and no function or trait definitions.
acceptance.evidence: crates/liminal/src/pressure/mod.rs:1-5 contains only pub mod capacity, pub mod signal, and pub use re-exports; no fn or trait definitions are present.

acceptance.criterion: PressureSignal, ConsumerCapacity, and CapacityTracker are accessible via use crate::pressure::{PressureSignal, ConsumerCapacity, CapacityTracker}.

Tests pressure::capacity::tests::pressure_root_re_exports_capacity_types and pressure::signal::tests::pressure_root_re_exports_signal_result_types import through crate::pressure and passed.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement pressure policies or policy configuration — those belong to ROUTING-006.
SHALL NOT implement pressure monitoring or metric aggregation — those belong to ROUTING-006.
SHALL NOT implement policy enforcement or automatic overload responses — those belong to ROUTING-006.
SHALL NOT define routing predicates or the routing table — those belong to ROUTING-001 and ROUTING-002.
SHALL NOT impose default capacity values — consumers must declare capacity explicitly.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal --all-targets -- -D warnings passes clean.
cargo test -p liminal passes with all pressure signal and capacity tracking tests green.
signal.rs and capacity.rs are each under 500 lines including tests (CN1).
pressure/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use — no logic.
